root_text.an3.59:1.1: Atha kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ …pe… ekamantaṁ nisinno kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.59:1.2: “yassassu, bho gotama, yañño vā saddhaṁ vā thālipāko vā deyyadhammaṁ vā, tevijjesu brāhmaṇesu dānaṁ dadeyyā”ti.
root_text.an3.59:1.3: “Yathā kathaṁ pana, brāhmaṇa, brāhmaṇā tevijjaṁ paññapentī”ti?
root_text.an3.59:1.4: “Idha kho, bho gotama, brāhmaṇo ubhato sujāto hoti mātito ca pitito ca saṁsuddhagahaṇiko yāva sattamā pitāmahayugā akkhitto anupakkuṭṭho jātivādena, ajjhāyako mantadharo, tiṇṇaṁ vedānaṁ pāragū sanighaṇḍukeṭubhānaṁ sākkharappabhedānaṁ itihāsapañcamānaṁ, padako, veyyākaraṇo, lokāyatamahāpurisalakkhaṇesu anavayoti.
root_text.an3.59:1.5: Evaṁ kho, bho gotama, brāhmaṇā tevijjaṁ paññapentī”ti.
root_text.an3.59:2.1: “Aññathā kho, brāhmaṇa, brāhmaṇā brāhmaṇaṁ tevijjaṁ paññapenti, aññathā ca pana ariyassa vinaye tevijjo hotī”ti.
root_text.an3.59:2.2: “Yathā kathaṁ pana, bho gotama, ariyassa vinaye tevijjo hoti?
root_text.an3.59:2.3: Sādhu me bhavaṁ gotamo tathā dhammaṁ desetu yathā ariyassa vinaye tevijjo hotī”ti.
root_text.an3.59:2.4: “Tena hi, brāhmaṇa, suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an3.59:2.5: “Evaṁ, bho”ti kho jāṇussoṇi brāhmaṇo bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an3.59:4.1: So evaṁ samāhite citte parisuddhe pariyodāte anaṅgaṇe vigatūpakkilese mudubhūte kammaniye ṭhite āneñjappatte pubbenivāsānussatiñāṇāya cittaṁ abhininnāmeti.
root_text.an3.59:4.2: So anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ dvepi jātiyo …pe… iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati.
root_text.an3.59:4.4: avijjā vihatā, vijjā uppannā; tamo vihato, āloko uppanno yathā taṁ appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato.
root_text.an3.59:5.1: So evaṁ samāhite citte parisuddhe pariyodāte anaṅgaṇe vigatūpakkilese mudubhūte kammaniye ṭhite āneñjappatte sattānaṁ cutūpapātañāṇāya cittaṁ abhininnāmeti.
root_text.an3.59:5.2: So dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena …pe… yathākammūpage satte pajānāti.
root_text.an3.59:5.4: avijjā vihatā, vijjā uppannā; tamo vihato, āloko uppanno yathā taṁ appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharato.
root_text.an3.59:6.1: So evaṁ samāhite citte parisuddhe pariyodāte anaṅgaṇe vigatūpakkilese mudubhūte kammaniye ṭhite āneñjappatte āsavānaṁ khayañāṇāya cittaṁ abhininnāmeti.
root_text.an3.59:6.2: So ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti;
root_text.an3.59:6.3: ‘ime āsavā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti …pe… ‘ayaṁ āsavanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.59:6.4: Tassa evaṁ jānato evaṁ passato kāmāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, bhavāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati, avijjāsavāpi cittaṁ vimuccati;
root_text.an3.59:6.6: ‘Khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāti.
root_text.an3.59:6.8: avijjā vihatā, vijjā uppannā; tamo vihato, āloko uppanno yathā taṁ appamattassa ātāpino pahitattassa viharatoti.
root_text.an3.59:10.1: Evaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, ariyassa vinaye tevijjo hotī”ti.
root_text.an3.59:10.2: “Aññathā, bho gotama, brāhmaṇānaṁ tevijjo, aññathā ca pana ariyassa vinaye tevijjo hoti.
root_text.an3.59:10.3: Imassa ca, bho gotama, ariyassa vinaye tevijjassa brāhmaṇānaṁ tevijjo kalaṁ nāgghati soḷasiṁ.
root_text.an3.59:11.2: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.an3.59:1.1: Then the brahmin Jānussoṇi went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him. Seated to one side he said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an3.59:1.2: “Worthy Gotama, whoever has a sacrifice, an offering of food for ancestors, a dish of milk-rice prepared for an auspicious ceremony, or a gift to give, should give it to the brahmins who have mastered the three Vedic knowledges.”
translation_text.an3.59:1.3: “But brahmin, how do the brahmins describe a brahmin who is proficient in the three Vedic knowledges?”
translation_text.an3.59:1.4: “Worthy Gotama, it’s when a brahmin is well born on both his mother’s and father’s side, of pure descent, with irrefutable and impeccable genealogy back to the seventh paternal generation. He recites and remembers the hymns, and has mastered the three Vedas, together with their vocabularies and ritual performance, their phonology and word classification, and the testaments as fifth. He knows them word-by-word, and their grammar. He is well versed in cosmology and the marks of a great man.
translation_text.an3.59:1.5: That’s how the brahmins describe a brahmin who is proficient in the three Vedic knowledges.”
translation_text.an3.59:2.1: “Brahmin, a master of three knowledges according to the brahmins is quite different from a master of the three knowledges in the training of the Noble One.”
translation_text.an3.59:2.2: “But worthy Gotama, how is one a master of the three knowledges in the training of the Noble One?
translation_text.an3.59:2.3: Worthy Gotama, please teach me this.”
translation_text.an3.59:2.4: “Well then, brahmin, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an3.59:3.1: “Brahmin, it’s when a mendicant, quite secluded from sensual pleasures …
translation_text.an3.59:3.2: enters and remains in the fourth absorption.
translation_text.an3.59:4.1: When their mind has become immersed in samādhi like this—purified, bright, flawless, rid of corruptions, pliable, workable, steady, and imperturbable—they extend it toward recollection of past lives.
translation_text.an3.59:4.2: They recollect many kinds of past lives, with features and details.
translation_text.an3.59:4.3: This is the first knowledge that they attain.
translation_text.an3.59:4.4: Ignorance is banished and knowledge has arisen; darkness is banished and light has arisen, as happens for a meditator who is diligent, keen, and resolute.
translation_text.an3.59:5.1: When their mind has become immersed in samādhi like this—purified, bright, flawless, rid of corruptions, pliable, workable, steady, and imperturbable—they extend it toward knowledge of the death and rebirth of sentient beings.
translation_text.an3.59:5.2: With clairvoyance that is purified and surpasses the human, they understand how sentient beings pass on according to their deeds.
translation_text.an3.59:5.3: This is the second knowledge that they attain.
translation_text.an3.59:5.4: Ignorance is banished and knowledge has arisen; darkness is banished and light has arisen, as happens for a meditator who is diligent, keen, and resolute.
translation_text.an3.59:6.1: When their mind has become immersed in samādhi like this—purified, bright, flawless, rid of corruptions, pliable, workable, steady, and imperturbable—they extend it toward knowledge of the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an3.59:6.2: They truly understand: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.an3.59:6.3: They truly understand: ‘These are defilements’ … ‘This is the origin of defilements’ … ‘This is the cessation of defilements’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of defilements’.
translation_text.an3.59:6.4: Knowing and seeing like this, their mind is freed from the defilements of sensuality, desire to be reborn, and ignorance.
translation_text.an3.59:6.5: When they’re freed, they know they’re freed.
translation_text.an3.59:6.6: They understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’
translation_text.an3.59:6.7: This is the third knowledge that they attain.
translation_text.an3.59:6.8: Ignorance is banished and knowledge has arisen; darkness is banished and light has arisen, as happens for a meditator who is diligent, keen, and resolute.
translation_text.an3.59:7.1: One who is perfect in precepts and observances,
translation_text.an3.59:8.2: sees heaven and places of loss,
translation_text.an3.59:8.3: and has attained the end of rebirth,
translation_text.an3.59:8.4: such a sage has perfect insight.
translation_text.an3.59:9.2: a brahmin is a master of the three knowledges.
translation_text.an3.59:9.3: That’s who I call a three-knowledge master,
translation_text.an3.59:9.4: and not the other <j>who repeats what they are told.
translation_text.an3.59:10.1: This, brahmin, is a master of the three knowledges in the training of the Noble One.”
translation_text.an3.59:10.2: “Worthy Gotama, the master of three knowledges according to the brahmins is quite different from a master of the three knowledges in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an3.59:10.3: And, worthy Gotama, a master of three knowledges according to the brahmins is not worth a sixteenth part of a master of the three knowledges in the training of the Noble One.
translation_text.an3.59:11.2: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
